description: Help Center & FAQ - Build a self-service help center with MDX articles organized by category, full-text search, getting-started guides, and a pre-sale FAQ page

userStories.title: Help article MDX infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need help article content processing so that articles can be authored as MDX files organized by category.
acceptanceCriteria: Content directory created at content/help/ with subdirectories per category: getting-started, calendar-scheduling, job-management, fleet-trucks, booking-requests, customer-communications, invoicing-xero, mobile-app, team-chat, account-billing
Frontmatter schema defined and typed: title (string), description (string), category (string slug), sortOrder (number), lastUpdated (string ISO date), tags (string array)
Utility function getAllHelpArticles(): reads all help articles grouped by category, sorted by sortOrder within each category
Utility function getHelpArticleBySlug(categorySlug, articleSlug): reads a single help article, returns typed article with MDX content
Utility function getHelpCategories(): returns category metadata (name, slug, description, icon, article count)

Shared MDX components from blog (Callout, ImageWithCaption, VideoEmbed) plus help-specific components: StepByStep (numbered steps with content), UIPath (breadcrumb-style path e.g. 'Settings → Integrations → Xero'), KeyboardShortcut (renders keyboard shortcut badges e.g. Ctrl+S)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on PW-05 for shared MDX infrastructure and base components. Help articles extend the same processing pipeline.

userStories.title: Sample help articles — Getting Started
userStories.description: As a developer, I need sample help articles so that the help center pages can be developed and tested with real content.
acceptanceCriteria: Create content/help/getting-started/setting-up-your-account.mdx: full frontmatter, ~300 words of step-by-step content covering company details, timezone, GST setup
Create content/help/getting-started/adding-your-trucks.mdx: full frontmatter, ~300 words covering truck creation with colors and capacity
Both articles use custom MDX components: at least one Callout and one StepByStep component
Create content/help/invoicing-xero/connecting-xero.mdx: sample article in a different category, ~300 words covering OAuth setup
All articles have realistic, helpful content — not lorem ipsum
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are the minimum articles needed for development. The full getting-started series (8 articles per MD) can be expanded later.

userStories.title: Getting started guide series content
userStories.description: As a new customer, I want step-by-step getting started guides so that I can set up VanMan for my business.
acceptanceCriteria: 8 getting-started articles created in content/help/getting-started/ with sequential sortOrder:
1. setting-up-your-account.mdx — company details, timezone, GST/tax number
2. adding-your-trucks.mdx — creating trucks with colors, capacity, team leaders
3. creating-your-first-job.mdx — walkthrough of job creation flow
4. setting-up-your-booking-form.mdx — embedding the public booking form on your website
5. connecting-xero.mdx — OAuth setup, account/item code sync
6. inviting-your-team.mdx — adding operators and drivers, role explanations
7. setting-up-the-mobile-app.mdx — downloading the app, logging in, role-based views overview
8. sending-your-first-sms.mdx — configuring Twilio, creating message templates
Each guide: numbered steps with screenshots/placeholders, callout boxes for tips and warnings
Each guide: 300-500 words of realistic, helpful content
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These guides complement the onboarding wizard from PW-04. They provide more detail for users who want deeper guidance.

userStories.title: Help center landing page
userStories.description: As a visitor or customer, I want a help center homepage so that I can quickly find the help I need.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /help
Search bar prominently positioned at top with placeholder text 'Search help articles...'
Category grid below search: one card per category with Lucide icon, title, article count, brief description
10 categories displayed: Getting Started, Calendar & Scheduling, Job Management, Fleet & Trucks, Booking Requests, Customer Communications, Invoicing & Xero, Mobile App, Team Chat, Account & Billing
'Popular articles' section showing top 5 articles (hardcoded initially, can be analytics-driven later)
'Contact Support' link at bottom of page linking to /contact
Page statically generated
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Help center landing page serves both pre-sale prospects evaluating VanMan and existing customers. Search bar is the primary interaction — make it prominent and fast.

userStories.title: Help category page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to browse articles within a category so that I can find the specific help article I need.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /help/[category-slug] — statically generated per category via generateStaticParams
Category heading with description text
List of articles within the category ordered by sortOrder
Each article shows: title, 1-line description, estimated read time
Breadcrumb: Help > [Category Name]
Sidebar navigation on desktop showing all 10 categories with current one highlighted
Mobile: sidebar collapsed into a dropdown/hamburger
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Sidebar navigation provides wayfinding across all help categories. Sort articles by sortOrder (not alphabetical) so the most important articles appear first within each category.

userStories.title: Individual help article page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to read a help article so that I can learn how to use a specific feature.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /help/[category-slug]/[article-slug] — statically generated via generateStaticParams
Article header: title (h1), last updated date formatted nicely
Article body rendered from MDX with rich formatting (headings, lists, images, callouts, step-by-step instructions)
Table of contents sidebar auto-generated from h2/h3 headings (sticky on desktop, collapsed dropdown on mobile)
Category sidebar navigation also visible on desktop (showing all categories with current category highlighted) alongside the article TOC
Active heading highlighted in TOC as user scrolls
Breadcrumb: Help > [Category Name] > [Article Title]
generateMetadata returns SEO metadata per article: title, description (from frontmatter description), canonical URL
JSON-LD HowTo structured data for step-by-step getting-started guides
'Related articles' section: 3 articles from the same category
'Still need help? Contact support' link at bottom linking to /contact
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Individual article pages are the workhorse of the help center. Category sidebar navigation should also be visible (not just TOC) so users can easily browse to other categories without going back.

userStories.title: Help article feedback widget
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to indicate if an article was helpful so that the team knows which articles need improvement.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Was this helpful?' widget at the bottom of each help article with thumbs-up (Yes) and thumbs-down (No) buttons
On click: shows 'Thanks for your feedback!' confirmation message with smooth transition
Feedback tracked as a client-side analytics event: trackEvent('help', 'feedback', { article: slug, helpful: boolean })
Buttons disabled after feedback given (prevents duplicate submissions via local state)
Widget styled consistently with the help article layout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Analytics event tracking integrates with PW-08 analytics system. No-op if analytics not yet implemented.

userStories.title: Help center search
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to search across all help articles so that I can find answers quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Search index JSON generated at build time from article titles, descriptions, headings, and body text
Client-side search using Fuse.js or FlexSearch loaded on the help center pages
Instant search results as user types (debounced at ~300ms) in the search bar
Results show: article title, category name, excerpt with highlighted matching text
'No results found' state with text 'No articles match your search.' and suggestion to contact support
Search works from both the help landing page and a search bar in the header on all /help/* pages
Search index loaded lazily (not blocking initial page load)
Search queries tracked as analytics events: trackEvent('help', 'search', { query: searchTerm }) to identify content gaps
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Search index can be reused for blog search as well. Help articles must be included in the site sitemap (handled by PW-08).

userStories.title: FAQ page
userStories.description: As a prospective customer, I want a FAQ page so that I can get quick answers to common questions before signing up.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /faq
FAQ organized into 6 sections with heading and accordion UI:
General (3 items): What is VanMan?, Who is VanMan for?, What countries do you support?
Pricing & Billing (4 items): How much does it cost?, Is there a free trial?, Can I cancel anytime?, What payment methods do you accept?
Features (4 items): Does VanMan have a mobile app?, Can I send SMS to customers?, Does it integrate with Xero?, Can my drivers use it?
Setup & Migration (3 items): How long does setup take?, Can I import data from my old system?, Do you offer migration help?
Security & Privacy (4 items): Where is my data stored?, Is it secure?, Is my data backed up?, Do you comply with NZ Privacy Act?
Support (3 items): How do I get help?, What are your support hours?, Do you offer training?
Each section expandable/collapsible accordion (shadcn/ui Accordion component)
Anchor links for each section heading (shareable direct links via URL hash, e.g. /faq#pricing)
CTA at bottom: 'Still have questions? Contact us' link to /contact + 'Start Free Trial' button to /signup
FAQPage JSON-LD structured data for Google rich results (all Q&A pairs)
Page statically generated
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: FAQ answers should be written in a friendly, clear tone. Link to relevant help articles and feature pages where appropriate.
